title: gomphrena pulverulenta
intro: Gomphrena pulverulenta, also known as dusty miller or powder poinsettia, is a type of flowering plant in the amaranth family, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and silvery-gray foliage. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin infections, and digestive issues. The health benefits associated with Gomphrena pulverulenta include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and support the body's natural defense mechanisms. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and resilience.
